data.title: Japan GDP Data Due On Monday
data.description:
BEIJING (dpa-AFX) - Japan will on Monday release preliminary Q4 figures for gross domestic product, highlighting a busy day for Asia-Pacific economic activity.

GDP is expected to add 0.4 percent on quarter and 1.6 percent on year after sinking 0.6 percent on quarter and 2.3 percent on year in the three months prior. Capex is seen higher by 0.8 percent on quarter after sinking 0.2 percent in Q3.

South Korea will provide January numbers for imports, exports and trade balance. In December, imports were up 11.7 percent on year and exports jumped an annual 33.9 percent for a trade surplus of $8.74 billion.

New Zealand will see January results for electronic card retail sales; in December, sales were down 0.1 percent on month and 0.5 percent on year.

Singapore will release January data for non-oil domestic exports; in December, NODX was down 9.4 percent on month and up 6.1 percent on year, with a trade surplus of SGD2.205 billion.

Thailand will provide Q4 numbers for gross domestic product, with forecasts suggesting an increase of 0.3 percent on quarter and 1.0 percent on year. That follows the 0.6 percent quarterly contraction and the 1.2 percent yearly gain in Q3.

Finally, the markets in South Korea, Taiwan, China and Indonesia are closed on Monday for the Lunar New Year holiday.

data.title: Genesis Minerals to Buy Magnetic For Roughly $452 Million
data.description:
(21:15 GMT) *Genesis Minerals Agrees to Buy Magnetic Resources
(21:17 GMT) *Genesis: Magnetic Shareholders to Receive A$1.40/Share Cash, 0.0873 Shares in Genesis Per Magnetic Share
(21:17 GMT) *Genesis: Offer Implies Value of A$2.00 Per Magnetic Share
(21:17 GMT) *Genesis: Offer Implies Fully Diluted Equity Value of Roughly A$639 Million
(21:18 GMT) *Genesis: Offer a 35% Premium to Magnetic 30-Day Volume Weighted Average Price
(21:18 GMT) *Genesis: Magnetic Shareholders Will Have Choice to Receive Offer Wholly in Cash, Scrip
(21:20 GMT) *Genesis: Magnetic Board Unanimously Recommends Deal
(21:20 GMT) *Genesis: Acquisition Expected To Be Completed in June
(22:38 GMT) Genesis Minerals to Buy Magnetic For Roughly $452 Million

By Rhiannon Hoyle

Genesis Minerals said Monday it has agreed to buy Magnetic Resources in a deal that values the gold explorer's equity at roughly 639 million Australian dollars, equivalent to US$452 million.

Under the offer, which has been unanimously recommended by directors of Magnetic, shareholders in the explorer will get A$1.40 in cash and 0.0873 share in Genesis for each Magnetic share.

While the default offer is roughly 70% cash and 30% scrip, shareholders can elect to receive the consideration wholly in cash or wholly in scrip, Genesis said.

The offer implies a value of A$2.00 per Magnetic share, a 35% premium to its 30-day volume weighted average price.

Magnetic's Lady Julie gold project will add more than 2 million troy ounces to Genesis' Laverton business, "further bolstering the mine life and production outlook," said Genesis Executive Chair Raleigh Finlayson. The Lady Julie project is next to land acquired by Genesis in its recent acquisition of Focus Minerals' Laverton gold project.

The deal is expected to be completed in June.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

data.title: FRW: Strong revenue and profit growth, margin gains, and strategic expansion via VTFE acquisition
data.description:
Half-year revenue rose 8.5% to NZD 720 million, with EBITA up 12.7% and NPAT up 17% year-over-year. Margin improvements, strong cash flow, and the VTFE acquisition position the group for steady growth, with ongoing focus on operational efficiency and targeted M&A.
